name: Gabriel Heatter
abstract: Gabriel Heatter (September 17, 1890 – March 30, 1972)  was an American radio commentator whose World War II-era sign-on, "There's good news tonight," became both his catchphrase and his caricature.

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: I had an original tune by the name of 'Gabriel Heater'. Now, that's one of the arrangements that I do that was from the head of the Music Department at RCA Victor. He put that together.
